qa_pairs.question: Is recompilation required for codes on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, codes that weren’t using the CVMFS software stack or that used absolute paths will have to be recompiled. It is recommended to recompile all your code, even if it did use the CVMFS stack on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: Which specific types of code require recompilation on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Codes that weren’t using the CVMFS software stack, or that used absolute paths, will have to be recompiled.

qa_pairs.question: Should all code be recompiled on Trillium, even if it used the CVMFS stack on Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is highly recommended to recompile all your code, even if it did use the CVMFS stack on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: Will virtual Python environments from Niagara work on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, your virtual environments will not work and you must recreate them because of the users’ directory change.

qa_pairs.question: Why do virtual Python environments need to be recreated on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You must recreate them because of the users’ directory change.

qa_pairs.question: What should users do if they previously used Anaconda virtual environments?
qa_pairs.answer: If you had Anaconda virtual environments, you will find that there is no Anaconda module. You should switch to using virtual environments instead.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the new quickstart guide for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The new quickstart guide for Trillium is located at [[Trillium Quickstart]].

qa_pairs.question: What is the support email address for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The support email for Trillium is trillium@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a self-guided course available for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a self-guided "Intro to SciNet and Trillium" course available at https://education.scinet.utoronto.ca/course/view.php?id=1389.
